diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with erythematous crusted papules over his scalp, eyelids, eyes, chin, and chest. Initial treatment for suspected folliculitis with oral amoxicillin/clavulanic acid showed some improvement, but new vesicles and crusted papules developed. Histology showed subepidermal bullous dermatosis with neutrophils and eosinophils. Direct immunofluorescence (DIF) showed linear BMZ deposition of IgA and fibrinogen, leading to the diagnosis of LABD. The resolution of skin disease after rectal pouch removal supported the diagnosis.
